treatments.name: Surgical Lobectomy with Mediastinal Lymph Node Dissection

evidence_level.rationale: Surgical lobectomy is supported by decades of phase 3 RCTs and is the NCCN/ESMO standard for resectable stage I NSCLC regardless of molecular subtype.

survival_benefit.rationale: Stage I NSCLC 5-year OS after lobectomy is 70-90% depending on substage. Cure rates are high for IA (>80%). Surgery offers the only proven curative intent for early-stage disease.

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at all major cancer centers. VATS lobectomy is standard. No biomarker or drug access barriers.

safety_profile.rationale: Operative mortality ~1-2% for lobectomy. Short-term morbidity rate ~50% (air leak, pain, atelectasis). ECOG 0 patient at 45 years old is an excellent surgical candidate. Long-term pulmonary function reduction is manageable.

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement for surgical eligibility. Applicable to all stage I NSCLC including ROS1-rearranged tumors.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical excision of the primary tumor and regional lymph nodes eliminates the localized malignancy. Lobectomy removes the entire lobe containing the tumor, achieving wide margins and enabling pathologic staging of lymph nodes.

key_evidence.study_name: Lung Cancer Study Group / LCSG 821 and subsequent meta-analyses
key_evidence.journal: Annals of Thoracic Surgery / Journal of Clinical Oncology

treatments.name: Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT/SABR)

evidence_level.rationale: STARS trial 10-year data (2025) showed comparable overall survival to lobectomy: OS 69% (SBRT) vs 66% (surgery). Phase 2 RCT with decade-long follow-up. Lung cancer-specific survival 92% vs 89%.

survival_benefit.rationale: 10-year OS comparable to surgery at 69% vs 66%. Lung cancer-specific survival slightly favored SBRT at 92% vs 89%. Non-invasive alternative with equivalent oncologic outcomes for T1-T2 tumors.

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at most radiation oncology centers. Non-invasive, typically completed in 3-5 sessions. No hospitalization required.

safety_profile.rationale: Rate of moderate-to-severe short-term side effects only 1% vs 50% for surgery in STARS trial. No operative mortality risk. Preserves pulmonary function better than lobectomy.

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirements. Applicable to all stage I NSCLC histologies and molecular subtypes.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Delivers highly focused, ablative doses of radiation to the tumor in 3-5 fractions using image guidance and motion management. Causes DNA double-strand breaks and tumor cell death while sparing surrounding normal lung tissue through steep dose gradients.

treatments.name: Taletrectinib (Ibtrozi) — Adjuvant or Salvage for Recurrence

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved June 2025 for locally advanced/metastatic ROS1+ NSCLC based on TRUST-I and TRUST-II phase 2 trials. ORR 88.8%, median PFS 45.6 months in TKI-naive patients. No adjuvant-specific data yet; TRUST-IV adjuvant trial is planned.

survival_benefit.rationale: Best-in-class PFS of 45.6 months and DOR of 44.2 months among TKI-naive patients. Intracranial ORR 76.5%. Most relevant as salvage therapy if recurrence occurs or as potential adjuvant (extrapolated from ALINA precedent with alectinib for ALK+ NSCLC).

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved as of June 2025. NCCN recommended as preferred first-line option for advanced ROS1+ NSCLC. Commercially available.

safety_profile.rationale: Most common TRAEs: elevated AST (70.2%), elevated ALT (66.5%), diarrhea (61.4%), nausea (44.3%). Grade 3+ TRAEs in 33%. Discontinuation rate 6.5%. TRK-sparing design reduces neurological toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for ROS1 fusion-positive tumors. Patient has confirmed ROS1 fusion+, making this a direct biomarker match. Also active against G2032R resistance mutation (61.5% ORR).

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Next-generation, highly selective ROS1 and NTRK tyrosine kinase inhibitor with potent CNS penetration. TRK-sparing design reduces off-target neurological toxicity. Retains activity against the common ROS1 G2032R resistance mutation through optimized binding to the kinase domain.

treatments.name: Repotrectinib (Augtyro) — Adjuvant or Salvage for Recurrence

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for ROS1+ NSCLC based on phase 1/2 TRIDENT-1 trial (NEJM 2024). ORR 79%, median PFS 35.7 months in TKI-naive patients. Phase 3 TRIDENT-3 trial comparing to crizotinib is recruiting.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS of 35.7 months and DOR of 34.1 months in TKI-naive patients — second only to taletrectinib. Intracranial ORR 89% in patients with measurable brain metastases. Active against G2032R resistance mutation (59% ORR).

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved and commercially available. NCCN preferred first-line option for advanced ROS1+ NSCLC.

safety_profile.rationale: Most common AEs: dizziness (61.3%, grade 1 in 73% of cases), dysgeusia (50%), paresthesia (30%). Discontinuation rate 7%. Generally manageable side effect profile.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets ROS1 fusion-positive tumors. Direct biomarker match for this patient. Also active against G2032R resistance mutation.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Next-generation ROS1/TRK/ALK tyrosine kinase inhibitor designed to overcome resistance to first-generation TKIs. Its compact macrocyclic structure enables binding to ROS1 kinase domain even in the presence of solvent-front mutations like G2032R. Achieves high CNS penetration.

treatments.name: Crizotinib (Xalkori) — Salvage for Recurrence

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for ROS1+ NSCLC based on PROFILE 1001 expansion cohort. Well-established evidence base with long-term follow-up. ORR 72%, median PFS 19.3 months, median OS 51.4 months.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 19.3 months, median OS 51.4 months (4-year OS 51%). Durable responses observed. However, PFS inferior to newer-generation agents (repotrectinib 35.7mo, taletrectinib 45.6mo). Limited CNS penetration.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since 2016 for ROS1+ NSCLC. Widely available globally. Longest track record among ROS1 TKIs. Generic availability improving access.

safety_profile.rationale: Most common AEs: visual disturbances (82%), GI effects (nausea, diarrhea, vomiting). Grade 3+ AEs are rare. Discontinuation rate <1%. Well-characterized long-term safety.

biomarker_match.rationale: First TKI approved for ROS1 fusion-positive NSCLC. Direct biomarker match. However, lacks activity against G2032R resistance mutation.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: First-generation multi-targeted tyrosine kinase inhibitor of ALK, ROS1, and MET. Competitively inhibits ATP binding to the ROS1 kinase domain, blocking downstream signaling via RAS/MAPK, PI3K/AKT, and JAK/STAT pathways. Limited CNS penetration compared to newer agents.

treatments.name: Entrectinib (Rozlytrek) — Adjuvant or Salvage for Recurrence

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for ROS1+ NSCLC based on integrated analysis of STARTRK-1/2/NGS trials. ORR 68-77%, median PFS 15.7 months. Phase 3 MO41552 trial vs crizotinib is recruiting. Also being studied in NAUTIKA1 perioperative trial.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 15.7 months, median OS 47.8 months, DOR 24.6 months. Intracranial ORR 80% among patients with CNS disease. Real-world PFS 19.0 months (superior to crizotinib's 8.5 months in real-world comparison).

accessibility.rationale: FDA and EMA approved. Commercially available worldwide. Being studied in perioperative setting (NAUTIKA1 trial) which is relevant for this stage I patient.

safety_profile.rationale: Neurological AEs (dysgeusia, dizziness, paresthesia), metabolic toxicities. Discontinuation rate 5%. Generally well tolerated for long-term administration.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets ROS1 fusion-positive tumors directly. Perfect biomarker match for this patient.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Potent CNS-active tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting ROS1, NTRK1/2/3, and ALK. Crosses the blood-brain barrier effectively, providing intracranial disease control. Inhibits the ROS1 kinase domain by competing with ATP binding.

treatments.name: Active Surveillance Post-Surgery (Without Adjuvant Therapy)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN guidelines recommend surveillance CT every 6 months for 2-3 years, then annual low-dose CT for completely resected stage I NSCLC. No proven benefit of adjuvant therapy for stage IA disease.

survival_benefit.rationale: Surveillance alone without adjuvant therapy is standard for stage IA. 5-year OS rates of 80-92% with surgery alone. Avoids unnecessary treatment toxicity. ROS1+ stage I recurrence rate is not well characterized but early detection enables effective TKI salvage.

accessibility.rationale: CT surveillance is universally available. Low cost compared to systemic therapy. No special testing beyond standard follow-up imaging.

safety_profile.rationale: No treatment toxicity — only radiation exposure from surveillance CT scans. Preserves quality of life. No drug side effects.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-specific. ROS1 status does not change surveillance recommendations for stage I disease. However, knowing ROS1 status enables rapid initiation of effective TKI therapy if recurrence detected.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Systematic imaging surveillance using CT scans at regular intervals to detect disease recurrence at an early, treatable stage. Enables timely initiation of targeted therapy (ROS1 TKI) or local treatment if recurrence is identified. Avoids overtreatment in patients with high cure rates from surgery alone.

key_evidence.study_name: NCCN NSCLC Guidelines v5.2025 / Surveillance Studies
key_evidence.journal: NCCN Guidelines / Journal of Clinical Oncology

notable_side_effects: Cumulative radiation exposure from CT scans

treatments.name: Zidesamtinib (NVL-520) — Investigational Next-Generation ROS1 Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 ARROS-1 trial data. NDA accepted by FDA with target action date September 2026. ORR 89% in TKI-naive, 44% in TKI-pretreated patients. Not yet approved but on track for approval.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 89% (TKI-naive), 12-month DOR rate 96% (TKI-naive). Best-in-class activity against G2032R resistance mutation (83% ORR). Median PFS 9.7 months in pretreated patients. Intracranial ORR 48-83%.

accessibility.rationale: Not yet FDA approved. NDA accepted with PDUFA date September 2026. Available through ARROS-1 clinical trial (NCT05118789). Expected commercial availability late 2026.

safety_profile.rationale: Very favorable safety profile: peripheral edema (36%), constipation (17%), elevated CPK (16%), fatigue (16%). Only 2% discontinuation rate and 10% dose reduction rate. Best-tolerated ROS1 TKI.

biomarker_match.rationale: Highly selective ROS1 inhibitor — most selective agent in the class. Direct biomarker match. Best-in-class activity against G2032R (83% ORR) and intracranial disease.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Highly selective, non-covalent ROS1 inhibitor designed to overcome limitations of existing TKIs. ROS1-selective design minimizes off-target kinase inhibition (avoids TRK, ALK), reducing toxicity. Potent activity against ROS1 wild-type and resistance mutations including G2032R and L2086F.

treatments.name: Platinum-based Adjuvant Chemotherapy (Cisplatin + Pemetrexed)

evidence_level.rationale: Adjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy is supported by multiple phase 3 RCTs and meta-analyses (LACE meta-analysis). Standard of care for stage II-IIIA. However, for stage IA, no benefit shown; for stage IB, benefit is marginal and limited to tumors >4cm.

survival_benefit.rationale: For stage I specifically, no proven OS benefit for IA and marginal benefit for IB (tumors >4cm only). Absolute 5-year survival improvement ~5% in stage II-III. Pemetrexed-based regimens show ORR 58% and PFS 7.2 months when used for ROS1+ recurrence.

accessibility.rationale: Universally available. Standard chemotherapy agents with well-established protocols. No biomarker testing required.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicities: nausea, myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity (cisplatin), fatigue. Treatment-related mortality ~1%. Four cycles required. Not trivial for a stage I patient where benefit is minimal.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-specific but pemetrexed has demonstrated particular efficacy in ROS1-rearranged tumors (ORR 58%, PFS 7.2 months in advanced setting). May be a reasonable option for recurrence.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin causes DNA cross-linking leading to apoptosis. Pemetrexed is a multi-targeted antifolate inhibiting thymidylate synthase, DHFR, and GARFT, disrupting nucleotide synthesis. ROS1-rearranged tumors appear particularly sensitive to pemetrexed, possibly due to lower thymidylate synthase expression.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / The Oncologist

treatments.name: Lorlatinib (Lorbrena) — Salvage for Recurrence

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1-2 data for ROS1+ NSCLC (not specifically FDA approved for ROS1, approved for ALK+). ORR 62% in TKI-naive, 35% in TKI-pretreated. IFCT-1803 LORLATU study showed ORR 45%, PFS 7.1 months. Smaller evidence base for ROS1 than crizotinib/entrectinib.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 21.0 months in TKI-naive (small n=21), 8.5 months in pretreated patients. Intracranial ORR 64% (TKI-naive) and 50% (pretreated). Notably lacks activity against G2032R resistance mutation (0% response rate).

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for ALK+ NSCLC but used off-label for ROS1+. Not NCCN preferred for ROS1+ first-line. Available but requires off-label use justification.

safety_profile.rationale: Neurocognitive effects, hypertriglyceridemia, hypercholesterolemia are notable. Real-world discontinuation rate 13% (higher than trials at 1%). Metabolic side effects require monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Active against ROS1 fusion-positive tumors but NOT active against G2032R resistance mutation. Good CNS penetration. Less targeted than ROS1-specific agents.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Third-generation ALK/ROS1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor with excellent blood-brain barrier penetration. Designed to overcome resistance to first- and second-generation ALK inhibitors. Potent against wild-type ROS1 but structurally unable to bind ROS1 G2032R mutant kinase domain.

treatments.name: Perioperative Entrectinib (NAUTIKA1 Trial)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 umbrella trial (NCT04302025) investigating perioperative entrectinib in ROS1/NTRK fusion-positive resectable stage IB-IIIB NSCLC. No published efficacy results yet. Analogous to ALINA trial paradigm for ALK+ disease.

survival_benefit.rationale: No direct survival data yet. Rationale based on entrectinib efficacy in metastatic setting (PFS 15.7 months) and ALINA precedent (adjuvant alectinib for ALK+ NSCLC improved DFS). Potential to reduce recurrence risk in early-stage ROS1+ disease.

accessibility.rationale: Available only through NAUTIKA1 trial at select sites including MSK and UCLA. Not standard of care. Requires trial enrollment.

safety_profile.rationale: Entrectinib safety is well characterized from STARTRK trials. Case reports support feasibility and safety of perioperative use. No unexpected toxicities in perioperative setting.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically enrolls ROS1 fusion-positive patients. Direct biomarker match for this patient. Stage I may be eligible depending on trial criteria (IB minimum).

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant entrectinib administered before surgical resection to achieve tumor shrinkage and eliminate micrometastatic disease, followed by adjuvant entrectinib post-surgery to reduce recurrence. Same ROS1/NTRK/ALK TKI mechanism as standard entrectinib.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Taletrectinib (TRUST-IV Trial — Planned)

evidence_level.rationale: TRUST-IV phase 3 adjuvant taletrectinib vs placebo trial is planned (NCT07154706) but not yet recruiting. No efficacy data. Based on rationale from ALINA trial (adjuvant alectinib in ALK+ NSCLC) and taletrectinib's best-in-class efficacy in advanced disease.

survival_benefit.rationale: No direct data. Extrapolated from taletrectinib's exceptional PFS of 45.6 months in advanced disease and ALINA precedent where adjuvant alectinib reduced DFS events by 76% in resected ALK+ NSCLC.

accessibility.rationale: Trial is planned but not yet recruiting. Not commercially available for adjuvant indication. Future availability uncertain. May take 3-5 years to complete.

safety_profile.rationale: Taletrectinib safety well characterized from TRUST-I/II. Known manageable profile with GI and hepatic toxicities. Appropriate for long-term adjuvant use.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for resected ROS1 fusion-positive NSCLC. Perfect biomarker match. Most directly relevant trial concept for this stage I ROS1+ patient.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Adjuvant administration of taletrectinib following complete surgical resection to eliminate residual micrometastatic ROS1-driven disease and reduce recurrence risk. Same mechanism as taletrectinib in advanced setting — selective ROS1/NTRK inhibitor with CNS activity.

treatments.name: Immunotherapy (Pembrolizumab) — Limited Role in ROS1+

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 data exists for pembrolizumab in NSCLC broadly (KEYNOTE-024/042/189), but ROS1+ subgroup analyses show minimal benefit. Consensus from oncology societies recommends against immunotherapy monotherapy in ROS1+ NSCLC.

survival_benefit.rationale: ROS1+ tumors show poor response to ICIs despite sometimes high PD-L1 expression. ORR to immunotherapy monotherapy in ROS1+ NSCLC is low. Chemo-immunotherapy may offer modest advantage but benefit attributed primarily to chemotherapy component.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple NSCLC indications. Widely available. However, NCCN specifically recommends against ICIs in ROS1+ NSCLC as first-line treatment.

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-related AEs (colitis, pneumonitis, hepatitis, thyroiditis). Important concern: prior ICI exposure may increase toxicity of subsequent TKI therapy, particularly with entrectinib or lorlatinib.

biomarker_match.rationale: Poor match for ROS1+ tumors. ROS1-rearranged NSCLC typically has low tumor mutation burden and tumor microenvironment unfavorable for immune checkpoint blockade despite variable PD-L1 expression.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 immune checkpoint, restoring T-cell-mediated anti-tumor immunity. In ROS1+ NSCLC, efficacy is limited due to low tumor mutation burden and an immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment characteristic of oncogene-driven tumors.
biomarker_requirements: Not recommended as first-line for ROS1+
notable_side_effects: Increased TKI toxicity with subsequent ROS1 TKI use
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (not recommended for ROS1+ first-line)

treatments.name: Cabozantinib (Cabometyx) — Off-label for Resistance

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and preclinical data only. Not FDA approved for ROS1+ NSCLC. Demonstrated activity against ROS1 G2032R resistance mutation in vitro (IC50 11nM) and in case reports. One case report showed PFS of 12 months as third-line after crizotinib and lorlatinib.

survival_benefit.rationale: Limited to case reports. One patient achieved 12-month PFS as third-line therapy. Also active against L2086F resistance mutation (case report). No systematic survival data available.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for other indications (RCC, HCC, MTC). Available off-label but requires oncologist justification. Not standard-of-care for ROS1+ NSCLC.

safety_profile.rationale: Known multi-kinase inhibitor toxicities: hypertension, diarrhea, hand-foot syndrome, fatigue, hepatotoxicity. More toxic than selective ROS1 inhibitors.

biomarker_match.rationale: Active against ROS1 fusions including G2032R and L2086F resistance mutations. Not a selective ROS1 inhibitor — also targets MET, VEGFR2, AXL, RET. Broader kinase profile.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-targeted tyrosine kinase inhibitor of MET, VEGFR2, ROS1, AXL, RET, and FLT3. Overcomes crizotinib/lorlatinib resistance in ROS1+ NSCLC by binding the kinase domain despite G2032R and L2086F mutations. Anti-angiogenic activity via VEGFR2 may provide additional benefit.

clinical_trials.title: TRUST-III: Taletrectinib vs Crizotinib in TKI-Naive Advanced ROS1+ NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: No results yet. Based on TRUST-I/II showing taletrectinib ORR 88.8% vs historical crizotinib ORR 72%.

clinical_trials.title: TRIDENT-3: Repotrectinib vs Crizotinib in TKI-Naive Advanced ROS1+ NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: No results yet. Based on TRIDENT-1 showing repotrectinib ORR 79%, PFS 35.7 months vs crizotinib PFS 19.3 months.

clinical_trials.title: MO41552: Entrectinib vs Crizotinib in TKI-Naive Advanced ROS1+ NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: No results yet. Comparing entrectinib (PFS 15.7mo) vs crizotinib (PFS 19.3mo) head-to-head.

clinical_trials.title: ARROS-1: Zidesamtinib (NVL-520) in ROS1 Fusion-Positive Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: TKI-naive ORR 89% (12-month DOR 96%). TKI-pretreated ORR 44% (median PFS 9.7 months). G2032R ORR 83%. Intracranial ORR 48%. NDA accepted, PDUFA September 2026.

clinical_trials.title: NAUTIKA1: Perioperative Entrectinib in Resectable Stage IB-IIIB NSCLC with ROS1/NTRK Fusions
clinical_trials.early_results: Case report data supports feasibility and safety of perioperative entrectinib in ROS1+ NSCLC. No systematic efficacy data yet.

clinical_trials.title: TRUST-IV: Adjuvant Taletrectinib vs Placebo in Resected ROS1+ NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: No results — trial not yet open. Modeled on ALINA trial design for adjuvant alectinib in ALK+ NSCLC.

clinical_trials.title: ALBATROS: Lorlatinib in TKI-Pretreated ROS1+ NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 34%, median PFS 7.4 months, median DOR 20.4 months. Moderate efficacy limited by lack of G2032R activity.

combination_strategies.rationale: For patients developing oligoprogression on ROS1 TKI, local ablative therapy (SBRT, surgery, or radiation) to progressing sites while continuing TKI allows extended disease control. NCCN endorsed approach for oligometastatic progression.

Pemetrexed-based chemotherapy shows particular efficacy in ROS1+ NSCLC (ORR 58%, PFS 7.2 months). Sequential use after TKI progression or as chemotherapy holiday between TKI lines may provide additional disease control. ROS1+ tumors appear to have enhanced pemetrexed sensitivity.

Sequential TKI strategy: start with crizotinib (if next-gen unavailable), then switch to repotrectinib (ORR 38%, PFS 9.0 months post-TKI) or taletrectinib (ORR 55.8%, PFS 9.7 months post-TKI) at progression. Both next-gen agents retain activity against G2032R resistance mutations acquired on crizotinib.

Zidesamtinib offers best-in-class activity against G2032R resistance mutation (ORR 83%) and other resistance mutations. Designed as a salvage option after progression on other ROS1 TKIs. Most selective ROS1 inhibitor available with minimal off-target toxicity (2% discontinuation rate).

Analogous to ALINA trial paradigm for ALK+ NSCLC. NAUTIKA1 and TRUST-IV trials are investigating perioperative/adjuvant TKI for resected ROS1+ NSCLC. Rationale: ROS1 TKIs could eliminate micrometastatic disease and reduce recurrence after curative resection.

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple studies and systematic reviews demonstrating improved pulmonary function, exercise tolerance, and quality of life in lung cancer patients undergoing lobectomy. Particularly relevant for this sedentary patient.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved FEV1 recovery, reduced post-operative complications, enhanced exercise capacity, faster recovery. Pre-habilitation may reduce operative risk and shorten hospital stay.

supportive_care.approach: Structured Exercise Program for Cancer Survivorship
supportive_care.evidence: ACSM guidelines recommend 150 minutes/week moderate aerobic exercise + 2-3 days resistance training for cancer survivors. Meta-analyses show reduced cancer-related fatigue, improved cardiovascular fitness, and potential survival benefit in NSCLC.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduction in cancer-related fatigue, improved cardiopulmonary fitness, enhanced quality of life, possible reduction in recurrence risk. Directly addresses patient's sedentary lifestyle risk factor.

supportive_care.evidence: While this patient has no reported smoking history (consistent with ROS1+ NSCLC demographics — typically never-smokers), counseling to avoid secondhand smoke exposure and maintain lung health is recommended by NCCN survivorship guidelines.
supportive_care.benefit: Preserves remaining pulmonary function post-lobectomy, reduces risk of second primary lung cancer, improves wound healing and recovery.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines recommend routine distress screening for all cancer patients. Young patients (45 y.o.) with cancer may have particular concerns about career, family, and long-term survivorship.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved adherence to surveillance protocols, reduced anxiety from follow-up scans ('scanxiety'), enhanced quality of life, better treatment decision-making.

supportive_care.evidence: Oncology nutrition guidelines support maintaining healthy BMI and Mediterranean-style diet for cancer survivors. Post-operative nutritional support improves healing and recovery. Sedentary lifestyle combined with adequate nutrition is important for post-surgical recovery.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved surgical recovery, maintained muscle mass, reduced inflammation, potential reduction in recurrence risk through weight management and anti-inflammatory dietary patterns.
